name: Immune Rejuvenation Coverage Repair Assessment for June 11, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 11 assessment found thin support-map, source, registry, limiting-evidence, review, safety, and taxonomy-boundary coverage for the moderate human_functional_benefit public outlook.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for a low-confidence, mixed-momentum human_functional_benefit public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages the missing mTOR/TORC1 older-adult trial anchors, the RTB101 phase 3 limiting evidence, a direct immune-aging registry, a recent immunosenescence/vaccine/mTOR/microbiota review, and an outlook support map that reuses existing TRIIM and spermidine records. Coverage is not strong because the evidence is branch-specific, the most mature program includes a phase 3 miss, active direct trials have no posted results, and long-term preventive safety and durability remain thin.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages three mTOR/TORC1 older-adult human trial records with vaccine-response, infection-rate, and respiratory-illness endpoints, and reuses existing spermidine and TRIIM human records as smaller adjacent immune-response and immunosenescence-marker branches.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The coverage is adequate for a narrow human-function stage, not strong enough for broad immune aging reversal.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged and reused records cover mTOR/TORC1 antiviral gene expression, PD-1-positive T-cell movement, TRIIM immunosenescence markers, spermidine autophagy-linked immune-cell markers, and review-level mTOR/microbiome/immunosenescence context.
The RTB101 phase 3 miss is staged as the main limiting human-functional evidence, and no-results GIANTS-1 and TRIIM-X registry context prevents over-reading active trial activity.

The mTOR/TORC1 trials report short-term tolerability and the active registries include safety endpoints, but chronic preventive immune, cytokine, endocrine, and mTOR safety and durability are not resolved.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Upgrade only after longer, larger, preventive-use safety datasets post results for older adults.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages GIANTS-1 and rechecks TRIIM-X and a withdrawn RTB101 follow-up; this is enough to return to ordinary surveillance rather than continue coverage repair.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue ordinary surveillance for GIANTS-1 and TRIIM-X status changes or posted results.

evidence_categories.rationale: PMID 42169620 is staged as a recent review connecting immunosenescence, vaccine efficacy, gut microbiota, and mTOR signaling while explicitly not counting as efficacy evidence.
The mTOR/TORC1 older-adult trial sequence, TRIIM pilot, and 2026 spermidine vaccine-response pilot are now visible in the support map as the current public anchors.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The anchors justify a narrow stage assignment but still require low confidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged outlook separates immune rejuvenation from mTOR/rapalogs, autophagy/spermidine, microbiome/synbiotic, thymic/endocrine TRIIM, vaccine-product efficacy, telomerase, and broad geroscience claims.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future stronger claims should split branch-level support maps before upgrading confidence.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair checked PubMed primary and review records for the mTOR/TORC1, spermidine, TRIIM, vaccine-response, and telomerase-adjacent literature; checked ClinicalTrials.gov for GIANTS-1, TRIIM-X, and withdrawn RTB101 follow-up; staged records that materially repair source map, support map, registry, limiting-evidence, review, and taxonomy-boundary gaps; and left adjacent vaccine-product, telomerase, and generic rapalog context out of the staged public map.
source_selection_notes: PMID 25540326, PMID 29997249, and PMID 33977284 were staged because they are the core older-adult mTOR/TORC1 immune-function trial sequence.
PMID 42169620 was staged because it provides recent review and taxonomy-boundary context across immunosenescence, vaccine response, mTOR, and microbiome mechanisms.
NCT07363057 was staged because it is a direct recruiting immune-aging trial with immune-cell, safety, function, cognition, health-event, and microbiome endpoints.
Existing TRIIM and spermidine result records were reused in the support map instead of restaged because they already exist in the live evidence layer.
NCT04139915, PMID 42228420, PMID 37086366, and NCT05237687 were checked but excluded from staging as zero-enrollment, vaccine-product, telomerase/disease-context, or generic rapalog context.

known_gaps.description: No staged branch has replicated durable older-adult immune functional benefit across larger successful trials; RTB101 in particular has a phase 3 miss.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Track independent mTOR/TORC1, vaccine-response, infection-prevention, TRIIM-X, GIANTS-1, and spermidine follow-up results before considering any confidence upgrade.

known_gaps.description: Long-term preventive use remains undercovered for mTOR inhibitors, immunocytokines, endocrine-thymic regimens, and immune-active supplements in older adults.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require longer safety follow-up, adverse-event reporting, and durability evidence before public wording implies practical preventive use.

known_gaps.description: Immune-rejuvenation evidence overlaps mTOR/rapalogs, autophagy, microbiome, endocrine/thymic restoration, telomerase, and vaccine-product efficacy.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep branch-specific support maps current and avoid upgrading the whole track from a single adjacent mechanism.

next_coverage_action: Review the staged coverage-repair bundle. If approved and published, return Immune Rejuvenation to ordinary surveillance with watch items for GIANTS-1, TRIIM-X, and any new mTOR/TORC1 immune-function program results.
